title: Well-definedness: theta'(F):=theta(F^perp) satisfies the essential relations (uses modularity)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Define $\Phi(\theta)=\theta'$ by $\theta'(F):=\theta(F^\perp)$. One must prove $\theta'$ is
again a genuine valuated matroid quotient of the trivial valuation on $M$, i.e. it satisfies the
essential relations of Prop. essential-relations.

This is the step where **modularity** is essential. The only hypothesis is that
$F\mapsto F^\perp$ is an order-reversing involution on $\mathcal{L}(M)$. From this one must derive
and then use:
- the **De Morgan identities** $(F\vee G)^\perp = F^\perp\wedge G^\perp$ and
  $(F\wedge G)^\perp = F^\perp\vee G^\perp$ (prove these follow from "order-reversing involution"),
- the rank complementation $\mathrm{rk}(F)+\mathrm{rk}(F^\perp)=r$,
- modularity $\mathrm{rk}(F)+\mathrm{rk}(G)=\mathrm{rk}(F\vee G)+\mathrm{rk}(F\wedge G)$,
to show that $\perp$ carries each essential relation for rank $k$ to an essential relation for the
complementary rank. The nontrivial difficulty: showing the bare lattice anti-involution interacts
correctly with the combinatorics (modular pairs) governing the tropical relations.
